Vector Space and Theological Meaning

CTSB-100 v2 draft audit of secular semantic priors in large language model embeddings. Version 2 compares four query contexts: bare/minimal, ordinary lived usage, neutral academic usage, and explicit Catholic/theological usage.

100
Concept-level units
1000
Query rows
-0.0202
Mean bare CAS
0.0234
Mean ordinary CAS
0.0525
Mean academic CAS
0.1113
Mean Catholic CAS
0.0880
Ordinary → Catholic shift
0.0589
Academic → Catholic shift
63.33%
Catholic top-rate: ordinary
92.00%
Catholic top-rate: Catholic context
1000
Unique query strings
Methodological note: Positive CAS means the query is closer to Catholic-magisterial descriptors. Negative CAS means the query is closer to secular/common-language descriptors. The main v2 shift metric is ordinary lived usage to explicit Catholic context. UMAP is illustrative only; substantive interpretation should rely on high-dimensional cosine, rank-order metrics, confidence intervals, and effect sizes.
Benchmark status: CTSB-100 v2 is a draft benchmark. Version 1 has been archived as an iterative starting point. Version 2 is stronger because it separates bare, ordinary, academic, and explicit Catholic query contexts. The descriptor sets should still be reviewed before final dissertation claims.
